name: openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG
description: The goal of sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem is to build an open, unified, and extensible system command service platform, driving the continuous evolution of commands from "tools" to "services", and ultimately to "intelligent interfaces".

name: openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG Repository List
description: openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG maintains 7 repositories in the Infrastructure and Tools domain

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG (Special Interest Group) is responsible for The goal of sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem is to build an open, unified, and extensible system command service platform, driving the continuous evolution of commands from "tools" to "services", and ultimately to "intelligent interfaces".. The SIG has 23 core members and maintains 7 repositories in the Infrastructure and Tools domain.

mainEntity.name: How to participate in openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: To participate in openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG: 1) Submit Pull Requests to relevant repositories; 2) Subscribe to the mailing list at sig-mcp-tools-ecosystem@openeuler.org; 3) Attend SIG regular meetings, see the openEuler website calendar for details.

mainEntity.name: What repositories does openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG include?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG maintains 7 repositories. Full list at https://www.openeuler.org/en/sig/sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem.

mainEntity.name: Who are the Maintainers of openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler sig-MCP-Tools-Ecosystem SIG has 6 Maintainers, including: Jiang Shaotao, Qin Yong, Fu Guangzhe. Maintainers are responsible for final code review and merging.
